name: Psychological Paper Movie Rough Draft Assignment
hints: Course → Psychological Paper Movie Rough Draft Assignment. Choose: Multiple Personality Disorder Documentary OR Stanford Prison Experiment (both free to watch — links in Blackboard).
notes:
25 pts — ROUGH DRAFT. MLA or APA format, Times New Roman, double-spaced, 1-inch margins. Answer ALL 5 questions (each worth 10 pts for final):
Q1: Introduce movie — plot, characters, why you chose it, psychology interests.
Q2: Pick ONE textbook chapter (Myers & DeWall 2024) that fits — use min 5 key terms.
Q3: How is mental health/addiction depicted? 2 examples from movie.
Q4: How do sociocultural influences impact characters/plot? Min 3 examples.
Q5: What is fact vs Hollywood exaggeration? Min 3 examples.
Include Works Cited/References page with textbook citation.

Week 4 Discussion Forum
hints: Course → Discussion Board → Week 4. Topic: Social Construction of Reality. Why do people believe things without evidence? How do family/media/friends shape beliefs? Give a real-life example of a false belief. Min 1 paragraph (5-7 sentences). Reply to 1 classmate.
notes: 20 pts. Post + reply to 1 classmate. Agree/disagree, add perspective, ask a question.

name: Exam 2
hints: Course → Assignments → Exam 2. Chapters 3-6. Use textbook and notes.
notes: 60 pts. Covers Chapters 3-6. Open book/open note. Review lectures, readings, discussions.

name: Week 5 Discussion Forum
hints: Course → Discussion Board → Week 5. Topic: Positivity & Encouragement. Share an inspirational quote that motivates you. Explain why it inspires you. Optional: share encouraging words for classmates.
notes: 20 pts. Post + reply to 1 classmate. Lighter week — motivational discussion.

name: Exam 3
notes: 65 pts. Review all remaining material. Use textbook and notes.

name: Week 6 Discussion Forum
hints: Course → Discussion Board → Week 6. Topic: Social Class & Life Chances. Does social class affect opportunities? Give an example of more/fewer opportunities based on background. Can people overcome these walls? Min 1 paragraph (5-7 sentences). Reply to 1 classmate.
notes: 20 pts. Post + reply to 1 classmate. Relate, add example, encourage classmate.

name: Read Chapters 6 & 7 + Watch Lectures
hints: Course → Content → Week 6 lectures. Watch Deviance and Crime videos + PowerPoint.
notes: Ch 6 (Deviance & Crime) and Ch 7. Review PowerPoints provided by instructor.

name: Week 7 Discussion Forum
hints: Course → Discussion Board → Week 7. Topic: Income Inequality. Watch the video first! What stood out? Do people start with same opportunities? How does social class affect life chances? Min 1 paragraph (5-7 sentences). Reply to 1 classmate.
notes: 20 pts. Post + reply to 1 classmate. Watch video first, then respond.

name: Week 8 Discussion — Social Norms Changing
hints: Course → Discussion Board → Week 8. Topic: Changing social norms. List 2 behaviors acceptable now but frowned upon 5-10 years ago. Why are they more accepted? Talk with a parent/grandparent about changes they noticed. Min 1 paragraph (5-7 sentences). Reply to 1 classmate.

name: Final — Movie Response Paper
hints: Course → Movie Response Assignment. Choose ONE: Where to Invade Next, Inequality for All, or Sicko.
notes:
120 pts — BIGGEST GRADE. 1.5-3 pages, double-spaced, 12pt font, title page, normal margins.
3 PARTS:
Part 1 (1-4 paragraphs): Summarize the documentary. Clearly identify which one.
Part 2 (2-4 paragraphs): Connect movie to 2 readings from textbook. Identify the 2 readings/topics.
Part 3 (1-2 paragraphs): Reflective independent thought stimulated by the video.
MUST include reference page with 3 acceptable sources. Cite ALL sources or automatic F.
Movie choices: Where to Invade Next, Inequality for All, or Sicko (2007).
Encouraged to watch the documentary more than once.

notes: Watch lecture videos and review PowerPoints for Chapters 4 & 5.

notes: EXTRA CREDIT. Worth doing for grade boost!

hints: Course → Week 4 → Data in the News Discussion. Find a recent article/infographic/data visualization about a real-world topic (economics, sports, polls, etc).
notes: Post must include: 1) Link or screenshot of visualization, 2) Explain it — what type of chart, what data, what trends, 3) Analyze — conclusions, biases, misleading aspects?, 4) Reflect — why you picked it, why stats matter. Reply to 2 classmates.
